cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /super-vibe 'build full auth system'. The initial scope completed successfully. metadata.design contains 3 phases. You're about to start the per-phase loop.

What do you do with the original scope task?

expectations.description: Marks the original scope task as completed — this prevents develop from finding the full-plan scope task when it runs per-phase re-scopes
Stores the phase list (titles and goals) in the tracker's metadata.phases so resume can access them without the original scope task

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /super-vibe 'add webhook system'. Initial scope produced 3 phases. Phase 1 (add webhook model) is complete and committed. You're starting phase 2 (add webhook delivery).

After creating the stacked branch for phase 2, what do you do before calling develop?

expectations.description: Invokes Skill('scope') for phase 2 — does NOT reuse the initial scope's design verbatim. The re-scope researches the actual codebase (which now includes phase 1's code) to produce a grounded design
The re-scope call includes context about which phases already landed and the original spec/prompt, so scope understands this is phase 2 of a larger plan
The re-scope uses --auto-approve so it doesn't pause for user review mid-pipeline

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /super-vibe 'add webhook system'. Initial scope produced 3 phases. Phase 1 is complete (branch: webhook-p1, committed).

How do you start phase 2?

expectations.description: Creates a new branch via Skill('gt:gt', args='create <slug>-p2') which stacks on the current branch (phase 1's branch), NOT on main
Proceeds immediately to phase 2 after phase 1 commits — no summary of phase 1 results, no user prompt, no pause

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /super-vibe 'refactor data layer'. 5 phases planned. Phases 1-3 completed successfully. Phase 4's develop stage fails completely.

What do you do?

expectations.description: Does NOT attempt phase 5 or submit — stops the pipeline at the failed phase
Does NOT update vibe_stage to 'phase-4' — keeps it at 'phase-3' so --continue resumes at phase 4
Reports which phases completed (1-3) and which failed (4), suggests /super-vibe --continue

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /super-vibe --continue. The tracker has metadata.super_vibe=true, status='in_progress', vibe_stage='phase-2', and metadata.phases contains 4 phases.

What phase do you start at?

expectations.description: Skips to phase 3 (the phase after vibe_stage='phase-2'), does NOT re-execute phases 1-2
Reads phase list from tracker's metadata.phases rather than re-running initial scope — the original scope task is already completed
